---
title: "MA611 Final Project"
author: "Uriel Ulloa"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: hide # Allows user to hide/show code chunks in the report
    theme: cosmo # A clean, professional theme
    highlight: tango
---

``` {r code}

## R CODE: Unemployment Rate Analysis (Updated Train/Test Split: Test starts 2021)

# =========================================================================
## 1. Setup and Data Loading (Retained)
# =========================================================================
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(tsibble) 
library(lubridate)
library(feasts) 
library(fable)
library(dplyr)
library(forecast)
library(fpp2)
library(ggplot2)
library(strucchange) 
library(prophet)
library(zoo) 
library(tibble) # For accuracy table creation
library(patchwork) # For plotting

# 1. Load the data files (UPDATE THESE PATHS)
midwest <- read_excel("/Users/urielulloa/Desktop/Time_Series_Project/Midwest.xlsx", sheet = "Monthly") %>%
  rename(Midwest = CMWRUR) 
northeast <- read_excel("/Users/urielulloa/Desktop/Time_Series_Project/Northeast.xlsx", sheet = "Monthly") %>%
  rename(Northeast = CNERUR)
south <- read_excel("/Users/urielulloa/Desktop/Time_Series_Project/South.xlsx", sheet = "Monthly") %>%
  rename(South = CSOUUR)
west <- read_excel("/Users/urielulloa/Desktop/Time_Series_Project/West.xlsx", sheet = "Monthly") %>%
  rename(West = CWSTUR)

# 2. Combine the datasets and convert to a tsibble (UNCHANGED)
unemployment_data <- midwest %>%
  left_join(northeast, by = "observation_date") %>%
  left_join(south, by = "observation_date") %>%
  left_join(west, by = "observation_date") %>%
  mutate(observation_date = ymd(observation_date)) %>%
  as_tsibble(index = observation_date)

# =========================================================================
## 2. Data Transformation and Preparation (UPDATED SPLIT - ERROR FIX)
# =========================================================================

df_unemp <- unemployment_data
start_date <- c(1976, 1)

# --- NEW SPLIT DEFINITION ---
new_train_end <- c(2020, 12)
new_test_start <- c(2021, 1)
new_test_end <- c(2025, 7) # Retaining the latest date from your previous test set


# 🚨 ERROR FIX: Create FULL Time Series (ts) Objects FIRST 🚨
midwest.ts <- ts(df_unemp$Midwest, start = start_date, frequency = 12)
northeast.ts <- ts(df_unemp$Northeast, start = start_date, frequency = 12)
south.ts <- ts(df_unemp$South, start = start_date, frequency = 12)
west.ts <- ts(df_unemp$West, start = start_date, frequency = 12)


# The forecast horizon h_horizon is now correctly calculated using the now-defined midwest.ts
h_horizon <- length(window(midwest.ts, start = new_test_start, end = new_test_end)) 


# --- Define New Training and Test Sets ---
train.midwest.FULL <- window(midwest.ts, end = new_train_end)
train.northeast.FULL <- window(northeast.ts, end = new_train_end)
train.south.FULL <- window(south.ts, end = new_train_end)
train.west.FULL <- window(west.ts, end = new_train_end)

test.midwest.FULL <- window(midwest.ts, start = new_test_start, end = new_test_end)
test.northeast.FULL <- window(northeast.ts, start = new_test_start, end = new_test_end)
test.south.FULL <- window(south.ts, start = new_test_start, end = new_test_end)
test.west.FULL <- window(west.ts, start = new_test_start, end = new_test_end)


# =========================================================================
## 3. COVID-19 XREG Impulse Creation (UPDATED SPLIT)
# =========================================================================

# Define the full time index
full_time_index <- time(midwest.ts) 

# Create the three impulse regressors for the peak months (April, May, June 2020)
impulse_apr <- ifelse(full_time_index == (2020 + 3/12), 1, 0) 
impulse_may <- ifelse(full_time_index == (2020 + 4/12), 1, 0)
impulse_jun <- ifelse(full_time_index == (2020 + 5/12), 1, 0)

# Combine them into a matrix for the full series
covid_impulse_train <- cbind(
  APR20_Impulse = ts(impulse_apr, start = start_date, frequency = 12),
  MAY20_Impulse = ts(impulse_may, start = start_date, frequency = 12),
  JUN20_Impulse = ts(impulse_jun, start = start_date, frequency = 12)
)

# Split the impulse matrix for the NEW TRAINING and TESTING periods:
# The impulse is ONLY in the training data, but xreg.test.FULL must have the 
# correct number of rows (all zeros) for the new forecast horizon.
xreg.train.FULL <- window(covid_impulse_train, end = new_train_end)
xreg.test.FULL <- window(covid_impulse_train, start = new_test_start, end = new_test_end)


# =========================================================================
## 4. Model Fitting: Naive, ETS, ARIMA, NNETAR (Uses new train/test sets)
# =========================================================================

print("--- FITTING MODELS TO FULL TRAINING DATA (Up to 2020-12) ---")

# --- Naive Model ---
naive.forecast.midwest.FULL <- naive(train.midwest.FULL, h = h_horizon)
naive.forecast.northeast.FULL <- naive(train.northeast.FULL, h = h_horizon)
naive.forecast.south.FULL <- naive(train.south.FULL, h = h_horizon)
naive.forecast.west.FULL <- naive(train.west.FULL, h = h_horizon)

# --- ETS Model (AAN) ---
ets.fit.midwest.FULL <- ets(train.midwest.FULL, model = "AAN") 
ets.fit.northeast.FULL <- ets(train.northeast.FULL, model = "AAN")
ets.fit.south.FULL <- ets(train.south.FULL, model = "AAN")
ets.fit.west.FULL <- ets(train.west.FULL, model = "AAN")
ets.forecast.midwest.FULL <- forecast(ets.fit.midwest.FULL, h = h_horizon)
ets.forecast.northeast.FULL <- forecast(ets.fit.northeast.FULL, h = h_horizon)
ets.forecast.south.FULL <- forecast(ets.fit.south.FULL, h = h_horizon)
ets.forecast.west.FULL <- forecast(ets.fit.west.FULL, h = h_horizon)

# --- ARIMA Model ---
# Find optimal non-seasonal orders (p, d, q)
best_order_midwest.FULL <- auto.arima(train.midwest.FULL, seasonal = FALSE, max.d = 1)$arma[c(1, 6, 2)]
best_order_northeast.FULL <- auto.arima(train.northeast.FULL, seasonal = FALSE, max.d = 1)$arma[c(1, 6, 2)]
best_order_south.FULL <- auto.arima(train.south.FULL, seasonal = FALSE, max.d = 1)$arma[c(1, 6, 2)]
best_order_west.FULL <- auto.arima(train.west.FULL, seasonal = FALSE, max.d = 1)$arma[c(1, 6, 2)]

# Fit and Forecast using XREG ONLY in the forecast step
arima.fit.midwest.FULL <- Arima(train.midwest.FULL, order = best_order_midwest.FULL, seasonal = list(order = c(0, 0, 0), period = 12), method = "CSS", include.mean = FALSE)
arima.forecast.midwest.FULL <- forecast(arima.fit.midwest.FULL, xreg = xreg.test.FULL, h = h_horizon)

arima.fit.northeast.FULL <- Arima(train.northeast.FULL, order = best_order_northeast.FULL, seasonal = list(order = c(0, 0, 0), period = 12), method = "CSS", include.mean = FALSE)
arima.forecast.northeast.FULL <- forecast(arima.fit.northeast.FULL, xreg = xreg.test.FULL, h = h_horizon)

arima.fit.south.FULL <- Arima(train.south.FULL, order = best_order_south.FULL, seasonal = list(order = c(0, 0, 0), period = 12), method = "CSS", include.mean = FALSE)
arima.forecast.south.FULL <- forecast(arima.fit.south.FULL, xreg = xreg.test.FULL, h = h_horizon)

arima.fit.west.FULL <- Arima(train.west.FULL, order = best_order_west.FULL, seasonal = list(order = c(0, 0, 0), period = 12), method = "CSS", include.mean = FALSE)
arima.forecast.west.FULL <- forecast(arima.fit.west.FULL, xreg = xreg.test.FULL, h = h_horizon)


# --- NNETAR Model ---
nnetar.fit.midwest.FULL <- nnetar(train.midwest.FULL, max.p = 12, max.P = 0, scale.inputs = TRUE)
nnetar.forecast.midwest.FULL <- forecast(nnetar.fit.midwest.FULL, h = h_horizon, PI = TRUE, npaths = 100)

nnetar.fit.northeast.FULL <- nnetar(train.northeast.FULL, max.p = 12, max.P = 0, scale.inputs = TRUE)
nnetar.forecast.northeast.FULL <- forecast(nnetar.fit.northeast.FULL, h = h_horizon, PI = TRUE, npaths = 100)

nnetar.fit.south.FULL <- nnetar(train.south.FULL, max.p = 12, max.P = 0, scale.inputs = TRUE)
nnetar.forecast.south.FULL <- forecast(nnetar.fit.south.FULL, h = h_horizon, PI = TRUE, npaths = 100)

nnetar.fit.west.FULL <- nnetar(train.west.FULL, max.p = 12, max.P = 0, scale.inputs = TRUE)
nnetar.forecast.west.FULL <- forecast(nnetar.fit.west.FULL, h = h_horizon, PI = TRUE, npaths = 100)


# =========================================================================
## 5. Accuracy and Model Selection (Consolidated Summary)
# =========================================================================

# --- Function to safely extract metrics (retained) ---
extract_metrics <- function(forecast_object, test_set, model_name, fit_object = NULL) {
  fail_df <- data.frame(Model = model_name, MAPE = NA, MASE = NA, RMSE = NA, AIC = NA)
  results <- tryCatch({
    acc <- accuracy(forecast_object, test_set)
    aic_val <- NA
    if (!is.null(fit_object) && inherits(fit_object, "ARIMA")) { aic_val <- fit_object$aic } 
    else if (!is.null(fit_object) && inherits(fit_object, "ets")) { aic_val <- fit_object$aic }
    if (nrow(acc) < 2) { 
      warning(paste("Accuracy output for", model_name, "is missing test set row!"))
      return(fail_df)
    }
    data.frame(
      Model = model_name,
      MAPE = acc[2, "MAPE"],
      MASE = acc[2, "MASE"],
      RMSE = acc[2, "RMSE"],
      AIC = aic_val
    )
  }, error = function(e) {
    warning(paste("FATAL ERROR encountered while processing", model_name, "forecast:", e$message))
    return(fail_df)
  })
  return(results)
}

# --- Compile Accuracy Summary for FULL Data Test (2021-01 onwards) ---
midwest_FULL_acc <- bind_rows(
  extract_metrics(naive.forecast.midwest.FULL, test.midwest.FULL, "Naive"),
  extract_metrics(ets.forecast.midwest.FULL, test.midwest.FULL, "ETS", ets.fit.midwest.FULL),
  extract_metrics(arima.forecast.midwest.FULL, test.midwest.FULL, "ARIMA", arima.fit.midwest.FULL),
  extract_metrics(nnetar.forecast.midwest.FULL, test.midwest.FULL, "NNETAR")
) %>% add_column(Region = "Midwest", .before = 1)

northeast_FULL_acc <- bind_rows(
  extract_metrics(naive.forecast.northeast.FULL, test.northeast.FULL, "Naive"),
  extract_metrics(ets.forecast.northeast.FULL, test.northeast.FULL, "ETS", ets.fit.northeast.FULL),
  extract_metrics(arima.forecast.northeast.FULL, test.northeast.FULL, "ARIMA", arima.fit.northeast.FULL),
  extract_metrics(nnetar.forecast.northeast.FULL, test.northeast.FULL, "NNETAR")
) %>% add_column(Region = "Northeast", .before = 1)

south_FULL_acc <- bind_rows(
  extract_metrics(naive.forecast.south.FULL, test.south.FULL, "Naive"),
  extract_metrics(ets.forecast.south.FULL, test.south.FULL, "ETS", ets.fit.south.FULL),
  extract_metrics(arima.forecast.south.FULL, test.south.FULL, "ARIMA", arima.fit.south.FULL),
  extract_metrics(nnetar.forecast.south.FULL, test.south.FULL, "NNETAR")
) %>% add_column(Region = "South", .before = 1)

west_FULL_acc <- bind_rows(
  extract_metrics(naive.forecast.west.FULL, test.west.FULL, "Naive"),
  extract_metrics(ets.forecast.west.FULL, test.west.FULL, "ETS", ets.fit.west.FULL),
  extract_metrics(arima.forecast.west.FULL, test.west.FULL, "ARIMA", arima.fit.west.FULL),
  extract_metrics(nnetar.forecast.west.FULL, test.west.FULL, "NNETAR")
) %>% add_column(Region = "West", .before = 1)

accuracy_summary_FULL <- bind_rows(midwest_FULL_acc, northeast_FULL_acc, south_FULL_acc, west_FULL_acc)

print("=========================================================")
print("### FINAL ACCURACY SUMMARY (Train: 1976-01 to 2020-12 | Test: 2021-01 onwards) ###")
print(accuracy_summary_FULL)
print("=========================================================")


# --- Model Selection (Winner) ---
best_models_FULL <- accuracy_summary_FULL %>%
  group_by(Region) %>%
  arrange(MASE, MAPE) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(Region, Model, MASE, MAPE)

print("--- WINNING MODEL PER REGION (Full Data Forecast) ---")
print(best_models_FULL)


# =========================================================================
## 6. Forecasting Plots (Four Separate Regional Overlay Plots)
# =========================================================================

# @title Helper function to combine and plot all four models for one region
create_regional_overlay_plot <- function(train_data, test_data, 
                                       naive_fc, ets_fc, arima_fc, nnetar_fc, 
                                       region_name) {
  
  p <- autoplot(train_data) +
    # Use window() to ensure test data is plotted correctly
    autolayer(test_data, series = "Actual Test", linewidth = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
    
    # Add all four model forecasts
    autolayer(naive_fc, series = "Naive Forecast", PI = FALSE) + 
    autolayer(ets_fc, series = "ETS Forecast", PI = FALSE) +     
    autolayer(arima_fc, series = "ARIMA Forecast", PI = TRUE, level = 95) + 
    autolayer(nnetar_fc, series = "NNETAR Forecast", PI = TRUE, level = 95) + 
    
    # Styling and Labels
    ggtitle(paste("Forecast Comparison for the", region_name, "Region (Test: 2021-01 onwards)")) +
    xlab("Year") + ylab("Unemployment Rate (%)") +
    guides(colour = guide_legend(title = "Series"),
           fill = guide_legend(title = "Prediction Interval")) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom", 
          legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
          plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))
  
  return(p)
}

# --- 1. Midwest Plot ---
P_Midwest_Overlay <- create_regional_overlay_plot(
  train.midwest.FULL, test.midwest.FULL, 
  naive.forecast.midwest.FULL, ets.forecast.midwest.FULL, 
  arima.forecast.midwest.FULL, nnetar.forecast.midwest.FULL, 
  "Midwest"
)
print(P_Midwest_Overlay)

# --- 2. Northeast Plot ---
P_Northeast_Overlay <- create_regional_overlay_plot(
  train.northeast.FULL, test.northeast.FULL, 
  naive.forecast.northeast.FULL, ets.forecast.northeast.FULL, 
  arima.forecast.northeast.FULL, nnetar.forecast.northeast.FULL, 
  "Northeast"
)
print(P_Northeast_Overlay)

# --- 3. South Plot ---
P_South_Overlay <- create_regional_overlay_plot(
  train.south.FULL, test.south.FULL, 
  naive.forecast.south.FULL, ets.forecast.south.FULL, 
  arima.forecast.south.FULL, nnetar.forecast.south.FULL, 
  "South"
)
print(P_South_Overlay)

# --- 4. West Plot ---
P_West_Overlay <- create_regional_overlay_plot(
  train.west.FULL, test.west.FULL, 
  naive.forecast.west.FULL, ets.forecast.west.FULL, 
  arima.forecast.west.FULL, nnetar.forecast.west.FULL, 
  "West"
)
print(P_West_Overlay)

# --- Combine all four final plots into a 2x2 grid for presentation ---
(P_Midwest_Overlay + P_Northeast_Overlay) / 
(P_South_Overlay + P_West_Overlay) +
  plot_annotation(title = 'Regional Forecast Summary: Models Trained on Data Before 2021', 
                  subtitle = 'Comparing Forecasts vs. Actual Data from 2021-01 onwards',
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold")))
```

## 9. Model Assumptions Check (Revisited)

### 9.1. ARIMA Model Diagnosis

```{r check_arima_assumptions, fig.height=8, fig.width=10}
# Check assumptions for the fitted ARIMA model (e.g., Midwest, Segment 3)
# This function generates 4 plots and performs the Ljung-Box test.
checkresiduals(arima.fit.midwest.FULL)
```

### 9.2. ETS Model Diagnostics

```{r check_ets_assumptions, fig.height=8, fig.width=10}
# Check assumptions for the fitted ETS model (e.g., Midwest, Segment 3)
checkresiduals(ets.fit.midwest.FULL)
```

### 9.3. Naive Model Diagnostics (White Noise Check)

```{r check_naive_assumptions}
# Check residuals for the Naive model fit
checkresiduals(naive.forecast.midwest.FULL)
```
